text: This video has been edited to a very small extent. The objective of this video is to demonstrate the most simple form of cholecystectomy performed through a single incision. This patient has been diagnosed with biliary dyskinesia. There are no signs of inflammatory tissue or previous infection.
The surgeon identifies the critical view of safety with ease due to the light areolar tissue and continues dissection along the hepatic fossa to ensure no aberrant anatomy is present.

After verifying the critical view, the surgeon places three clips on the cystic duct and three clips on the cystic artery, then transects these structures between the clips. The gallbladder is removed from the fossa using electrocautery, carefully dissecting the thin areolar plane between the gallbladder and liver to prevent bleeding and bile spillage.
The surgical bed is shown with the liver appearing hemostatic and the clips securely in place on the cystic duct and artery, indicating the completion of the operation.
